intents.subject: General
intents.response:
Here are all the subjects offered at the university:

1. Mathematics
2. Physics
3. Chemistry
4. Biology
5. Computer Science
6. Electronics
7. Mechanical Engineering
8. Civil Engineering
9. Electrical Engineering
10. English Literature
11. History
12. Economics
13. Business Administration
14. Psychology
15. Sociology
16. Political Science
17. Environmental Science
18. Statistics
19. Biotechnology
20. Data Science

You can ask me about the topics of any subject. For example: 'Show topics of Data Science' or 'What are the topics in Computer Science?'

intents.subject: All
intents.response:
When a student asks for topics of a specific subject, look it up in the 'subject_syllabus_qa' section and return the 'topics' array for that subject. Format the response as a numbered list. Below are all subject topics for reference:

Mathematics: Calculus, Linear Algebra, Differential Equations, Probability, Number Theory
Physics: Mechanics, Electromagnetism, Thermodynamics, Quantum Physics, Optics
Chemistry: Organic Chemistry, Inorganic Chemistry, Physical Chemistry, Analytical Chemistry, Biochemistry
Biology: Cell Biology, Genetics, Ecology, Human Physiology, Evolution
Computer Science: Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, DBMS, Networking, Machine Learning
Electronics: Circuit Theory, Digital Electronics, Microprocessors, VLSI, Signal Processing
Mechanical Engineering: Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Machine Design, Manufacturing, CAD/CAM
Civil Engineering: Structural Analysis, Geotechnical Engineering, Fluid Mechanics, Construction Management, Surveying
Electrical Engineering: Power Systems, Control Systems, Electrical Machines, Power Electronics, Renewable Energy
English Literature: British Literature, American Literature, Poetry, Drama, Literary Theory
History: Ancient Civilizations, Medieval History, Modern History, World Wars, Post-Colonial Studies
Economics: Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Econometrics, Development Economics, International Trade
Business Administration: Management, Marketing, Finance, HRM, Operations, Entrepreneurship
Psychology: Cognitive Psychology, Developmental Psychology, Social Psychology, Abnormal Psychology, Research Methods
Sociology: Social Theory, Research Methods, Gender Studies, Urban Sociology, Cultural Studies
Political Science: Political Theory, Comparative Politics, International Relations, Public Policy, Governance
Environmental Science: Ecology, Climate Change, Pollution Control, Environmental Policy, Sustainability
Statistics: Probability Theory, Statistical Inference, Regression, Time Series, Sampling Methods
Biotechnology: Molecular Biology, Genetics, Bioinformatics, Microbiology, Bioprocess Engineering
Data Science: Machine Learning, Big Data, Python for Data Science, Data Visualization, Deep Learning, NLP

intents.subject: General
patterns: What time does my lecture start?
intents.response: Your class schedule is available on the university portal under 'My Timetable'. Log in and navigate to Academics > Timetable to view your full weekly schedule.

intents.subject: Mathematics
intents.response: Mathematics lectures are typically scheduled on [Day] at [Time] in [Hall]. Please verify on the portal as schedules may vary each semester.

intents.subject: Physics
patterns: What time is my Physics lab?
intents.response: Physics theory classes and lab sessions are listed on your timetable. Labs are usually 2-hour sessions. Check the portal under Academics > Timetable > Physics.

intents.subject: Chemistry
patterns: What time is the Chemistry lecture?
Is there Chemistry practical this week?
intents.response: Chemistry classes include theory and practical sessions. Your lab schedule is on the portal. Safety guidelines must be followed during all Chemistry practicals.

intents.subject: Computer Science
patterns: What time is the coding class?
When is the CS lab session?
intents.response: Computer Science lectures and lab sessions (programming labs) are listed on the portal. Lab sessions require your student login to access the lab systems.

intents.subject: Biology
patterns: When is the Botany or Zoology class?
What time is my Biology lecture?
intents.response: Biology classes include theory, lab, and field sessions. Check your portal for the full Biology timetable including all lab practicals.

intents.subject: General
patterns: When does the new semester start?
What is the semester start date?
intents.response: The new semester start date is published in the Academic Calendar on the university website. Go to Academics > Academic Calendar to find semester start and end dates.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Class cancellations and holiday notices are posted on the portal under 'Notices' and sent to your registered email. Check the notice board for real-time updates.

intents.subject: General
patterns: When do I have to submit my project?
How many days left for submission?
intents.response: The general deadline for all subject assignments is May 05, 2026. Please check your LMS for subject-specific submission links.

intents.subject: Mathematics
patterns: When is the Math assignment due?
intents.response: The Mathematics assignment submission deadline is May 05, 2026. Please upload your work to the LMS before 11:59 PM.

intents.subject: Computer Science
patterns: When is the programming assignment due?
When to submit my software project?
intents.response: The Computer Science assignment submission deadline is May 01, 2026. Please upload your work to the LMS before 11:59 PM.

intents.subject: English Literature
patterns: When to submit my book review?
Research paper due date for English?
intents.response: The English Literature assignment submission deadline is May 01, 2026. Please upload your work to the LMS before 11:59 PM.

intents.subject: General
patterns: I need more time for my assignment
intents.response: Extension requests must be submitted to your course faculty at least 48 hours before the deadline. Go to the portal > Academics > Extension Request, or email your faculty directly with a valid reason.

intents.subject: General
patterns: How do I submit my assignment?
intents.response: Log in to the LMS > My Courses > Select Subject > Assignments > Click Submit > Upload your file (PDF/DOCX/ZIP as specified). You will receive an email confirmation after submission.

intents.subject: General
patterns: What if I miss the deadline?
Can I submit after the due date?
intents.response: Late submissions may result in a grade penalty (typically 10% per day) as per university policy. Contact your faculty immediately. In genuine emergencies, submit a late submission form via the portal.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: The general semester exams are scheduled from May 15, 2026 to June 14, 2026.

intents.subject: Mathematics
intents.response: The Mathematics exam date is listed in the semester exam timetable on the portal. The exam typically covers all topics from the syllabus. Bring your hall ticket and university ID.

intents.subject: Physics
intents.response: Physics has both theory and practical exams. Theory exam dates are in the main timetable. Practical exam dates are communicated separately by the Physics department. Check your portal.

intents.subject: Computer Science
patterns: When is the Computer Science exam?
When is the Data Structures exam?
intents.response: CS exams include both theory and practical (lab) exams. Theory exams are in the main timetable. Lab exams are scheduled by the CS department — check the portal for the lab exam date.

intents.subject: General
patterns: How to get exam hall ticket?
intents.response: Hall tickets are available on the student portal 10 days before exams. Go to Portal > Examinations > Hall Ticket > Download. If not generated, contact the exam cell immediately.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Results are announced within 4–6 weeks of the last exam. Go to Portal > Examinations > Results and enter your roll number. You will receive an SMS and email when results are published.

intents.subject: General
patterns: I want to recheck my answer sheet
intents.response: Revaluation requests must be submitted within 7 days of result announcement. Go to Portal > Examinations > Revaluation Request. A fee applies. Results of revaluation are final.

intents.subject: General
patterns: Can I bring calculator to exam?
Is mobile allowed in exam hall?
intents.response: Bring your hall ticket and university ID card. Scientific calculators are allowed only for specified subjects. Mobile phones and electronic devices are strictly prohibited in exam halls.

intents.subject: General
patterns: How much is the semester fee?
How much do I need to pay?
intents.response: Fee structures vary by programme and year. Log in to the portal > Finance > Fee Structure to view the detailed breakdown including tuition, lab, library, and other fees for your course.

intents.subject: General
patterns: When is the last date to pay fee?
When should I pay my fees?
intents.response: The fee payment deadline is published on the portal under Finance > Fee Payment. Late payment attracts a fine. Pay before the deadline to avoid academic restrictions.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Pay online via Portal > Finance > Pay Fee. Accepted methods: UPI, Net Banking, Credit/Debit Card. A payment receipt is emailed to you automatically after successful payment.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Merit and need-based scholarships are available. Visit Portal > Scholarships to see all available schemes, eligibility criteria, and application deadlines. Government schemes like NSP are also applicable.

intents.subject: Computer Science
patterns: What is the admission process for Computer Science?
How to apply for B.Tech CS?
intents.response: B.Tech Computer Science admission requires 60%+ in 10+2 (PCM/PCB) and a valid entrance score (JEE/state-level). Apply on the admissions portal when applications open. Cutoffs vary each year.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Admissions open in [Month]. Register on the admissions portal > Fill the application form > Upload documents > Pay the application fee > Track status online. You will be notified of selection.

intents.subject: General
intents.response: Download your fee receipt from Portal > Finance > Payment History. Click on any completed transaction and click 'Download Receipt'. This is an official document accepted for scholarship and tax purposes.

intents.subject: General
patterns: Who can apply for merit scholarship?
intents.response: Eligibility for the Merit-Based Scholarship requires a minimum GPA of 3.8. Need-Based Aid requires proof of household income below $40,000. Research Grants require a faculty-approved proposal.

qa.question: What topics are covered in Mathematics this semester?
qa.answer: Mathematics covers Calculus, Linear Algebra, Differential Equations, Probability & Statistics, and Discrete Mathematics as per the syllabus on the portal.

qa.question: Is Mathematics difficult?
qa.answer: Mathematics requires regular practice. Your faculty offers tutorial sessions and extra help. Visit the Math lab or attend doubt-clearing sessions listed on the timetable.

qa.question: What is covered in Physics this semester?
qa.answer: Physics covers Mechanics, Thermodynamics, Electromagnetism, Optics, and an introduction to Quantum Physics along with lab practicals.

qa.question: Is Physics practical compulsory?
qa.answer: Yes, Physics practical is a compulsory component with separate internal marks. Missing practicals without valid reason may result in attendance shortage.

qa.question: What is covered in Chemistry?
qa.answer: Chemistry covers Organic, Inorganic, Physical, and Analytical Chemistry. Lab sessions include volumetric analysis and synthesis experiments.

qa.question: What safety rules apply in the Chemistry lab?
qa.answer: Always wear a lab coat, gloves, and safety goggles. No food or drink in the lab. Follow disposal instructions for chemicals. Report spills to the lab instructor immediately.

qa.question: What subjects are taught in Computer Science?
qa.answer: CS covers Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, Database Management Systems, Computer Networks, and electives like Machine Learning and Web Development.

qa.question: Which programming languages are taught?
qa.answer: The CS programme teaches Python, Java, C/C++, SQL, and web technologies (HTML/CSS/JS). Electives may include R, Scala, or Go.

qa.question: What is covered in Electronics Engineering?
qa.answer: Electronics covers Circuit Theory, Digital Electronics, Microprocessors, VLSI Design, and Signal & Image Processing along with lab experiments.

qa.question: What subjects are in Mechanical Engineering?
qa.answer: Mechanical Engineering covers Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Machine Design, Manufacturing Technology, Engineering Materials, and CAD/CAM.

qa.question: What is taught in Civil Engineering?
qa.answer: Civil Engineering covers Structural Analysis, Geotechnical Engineering, Fluid Mechanics, Construction Management, Surveying, and Environmental Engineering.

qa.question: What subjects are covered in Electrical Engineering?
qa.answer: Electrical Engineering covers Power Systems, Control Systems, Electrical Machines, Power Electronics, and emerging areas like Renewable Energy and Smart Grids.

qa.question: What is studied in English Literature?
qa.answer: English Literature covers British and American Literature, Poetry, Drama, Short Fiction, and Literary Theory. Students write essays and present seminars.

qa.question: What citation style is used for English assignments?
qa.answer: MLA (Modern Language Association) format is the standard citation style for English Literature assignments. Refer to the MLA Handbook or Purdue OWL for guidelines.

qa.question: What topics are covered in Economics?
qa.answer: Economics covers Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Econometrics, Development Economics, and International Trade & Finance.

qa.question: What is studied in Business Administration (MBA/BBA)?
qa.answer: Business Administration covers Management Principles, Marketing, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Operations, Strategy, and Entrepreneurship.

qa.question: What topics are in the Psychology syllabus?
qa.answer: Psychology covers Cognitive, Developmental, Social, Abnormal, and Clinical Psychology along with Research Methods and Statistics for Psychology.

qa.question: What is taught in Data Science?
qa.answer: Data Science covers Python/R, Machine Learning, Big Data tools (Hadoop/Spark), Data Visualization, Deep Learning, and Natural Language Processing.

qa.question: What software is used in Data Science?
qa.answer: Students use Python (pandas, scikit-learn, TensorFlow), Jupyter Notebooks, Tableau, SQL, and cloud platforms like AWS or Google Colab.

qa.question: What is covered in Biotechnology?
qa.answer: Biotechnology covers Molecular Biology, Genetics, Bioinformatics, Microbiology, Immunology, and Bioprocess Engineering along with wet lab practicals.

qa.question: What topics are in Statistics?
qa.answer: Statistics covers Probability Theory, Statistical Inference, Regression Analysis, Time Series, Sampling Methods, and Applied Statistics using R or SPSS.
